ops.title: cco adopt: step-1 probe results (2026-07-18)
ops.body:
Live probes for the `cco adopt` plan (`/Users/yasyf/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-16/plans/we-need-an-adopt-attach-federated-fairy.md`), all on darwin, claude 2.1.214:

1. **Slug algorithm confirmed**: every non-alphanumeric char → `-` (verified `/` and `.`: `/Users/yasyf/.cc-orchestrate/worktrees/...` → `-Users-yasyf--cc-orchestrate-worktrees-...`). Claude records the **symlink-resolved** cwd (`/tmp/x` → `/private/tmp/x`, slug `-private-tmp-x`). Transcript entries carry `cwd`, `sessionId`, `isSidechain`, `gitBranch`, `version`; metadata lines with `type: last-prompt` / `type: attachment` interleave and may lack `cwd` — discovery's bounded parser must skip non-entry types (the FIRST line of a transcript can be `last-prompt`).
2. **SIGTERM confirmed**: idle interactive claude exits ~3s after SIGTERM, appends a final `last-prompt` metadata line (clean flush), last line parses. `claude --resume <sid>` restores the conversation and continues **in the same file with the same sessionId** (verified by size growth + single jsonl + unchanged sid).
3. **ccp foreign-sid confirmed**: `ccp run --resume <sid>` on a sid ccp never launched works — ccp proxies claude flags, account-switches (pool accounts symlink `projects/` → `~/.claude/projects`, so transcripts are shared), and the continuation appends to the same file/sid. Boot can be slow (session-start hooks); no fallback to bare `claude` needed — adopt keeps `claudeInvocation()`'s ccp preference.
4. **PID lister correction (plan §2)**: `lsof -a -c claude -d cwd` does NOT match the real process — claude is a native Mach-O whose lsof command name is not the bare string `claude` (ps comm shows the full path `/Users/yasyf/.local/bin/claude`). Working darwin approach, verified: enumerate via `ps` matching **basename(argv[0]) == "claude"** exactly, then `lsof -p <pid> -a -d cwd -Fn` for the cwd (`p<pid>/fcwd/n<path>` records). MCP children (cc-notes, ccx, cc-review, `cco channel`) are separate processes with their own names — the exact-basename rule already excludes them; `claude mcp serve` children remain the case the descendant-exclusion tier handles.
